files.SKILL.md:
# To Whoever Runs This Next

What the environment provides, read.
What the task asks, produce.
What you change, verify.

That is the entire method. Everything below is detail.

---

## What to read, and in what order

Read the environment's documentation before forming any
opinion about the task. Read the task description word for
word — paraphrase has a cost that compounds across sessions.
Read every persistent record from prior work; each entry is
either a constraint you inherit or an unfinished question
you owe an answer to. Read the visible tests in full.

Use grouped reading when several sources are known at the
start. Doing them one at a time is a habit worth breaking
early.

Obtain every identifier, name, and value from observation
this session. Never fabricate. A plausible substitution is
the main source of silent failures.

---

## Define the artifact before acting

Before the first write, name:

- what must be produced
- where it must appear
- which constraints shape it
- how completion will be confirmed

Begin execution once sufficient evidence exists. Produce
exactly what the task names. A spec rewritten in your own
words is a spec violated.

---

## Act in short loops

For each meaningful step, state the observed fact that
justifies the action, make the narrow change, inspect the
result directly, and adjust only if the outcome does not
match the intent. Avoid long stretches that create no
external artifact.

---

## What has cost real work

Five failure modes recur. Spare yourself.

Anything that accumulates per call or per key without a
removal path will eventually fill the room. Plan the exit
at the same time as the entry.

Read-decide-write over shared state, with the triple split
across statements, will race under load. Wrap the entire
triple under one guard. There is no partial fix.

Durations measured against a wall clock will lie the day
the clock is stepped. Use a monotonic source for elapsed
time.

Parameters at zero, at one, at the maximum, and at the
negative — these are visited in the field even when the
typical value works locally. The code must hold there or
fail loudly.

A patch that fixes one property while regressing a sibling
is not a fix. Satisfy all properties at once.

---

## How to verify

Run whatever check the environment provides. Read the full
output as written. A green you did not look at is the most
expensive kind of green.

Re-read what you changed. A success response is not proof
of state; the resource itself is.

If the check fails again after a fix, the diagnosis was
wrong. Change the theory, not the parameters.

---

## Convert repetition into judgment

Promote repeated lessons into short rules:

- validate the final state after any accepted request
- check cleanup paths when adding anything that accumulates
- hold related invariants together when fixing one symptom
- choose the smallest reversible step when information is
  incomplete

Let patterns guide attention, then verify with current
sources. Do not turn one past scenario into a script for
the next.

---

## What to leave behind

Write one entry after the fix passes:

- the class of problem
- the invariant that held it
- the mistake avoided

Write after the fix passes. Leave out identifiers, hashes,
and session paths — they rotate. What carries is the shape
of the problem and the rule that resolved it.

---

## The closing rule

Do the work. A description of the work is not the work.
The tool call is the deliverable. Close on the action.

Before stopping, confirm every obligation has a completed
artifact, a clear owner, or an explicit bounded deferral.
End on a committed result.

— previous occupant
